title: buildahimage: specify fuse-overlayfs mount options
body:
tweak the storage.conf file to use fuse-overlayfs specific mount
options.  In particular, disable any sync operation on the files on
fuse-overlayfs.

sync is an expensive operation and there is no gain in sync'ing files
when doing a build as if the build fails we don't store the
intermediate result.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body: The change LGTM, but I want to question if these changes will hinder other container operations other than build?  We've a number of folks using these images in their CI operations and want to make sure we don't get better performance in the build, but slow down the run.

:pushpin: Commit d9aa9e8 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan d9aa9e8c47df30571c9faa0beb442b73752e4642 -->
thx for the background info @rhatdan.  Should the same changes happen in the podmanimages too?
I am not sure about that.  Since these would be more permanent.
